Cookies.text: We use cookies to measure and improve your experience.
Cookies.necessaryCookiesDescription: These cookies are essential for the website to function properly. They enable basic functions like page navigation and access to secure areas of the website.
Cookies.analyticsCookiesDescription: These cookies help us understand how visitors interact with our website by collecting and reporting information anonymously. They help us improve our website and services.
Cookies.marketingCookiesDescription: These cookies are used to track visitors across websites. They are set to display targeted advertisements that are relevant and engaging for individual users.
Cookies.preferenceCookiesDescription: These cookies enable the website to remember choices you make (such as your username, language, or the region you are in) and provide enhanced, more personal features.
Cookies.howToManageDescription: You can set your browser to refuse all or some browser cookies, or to alert you when websites set or access cookies. If you disable or refuse cookies, please note that some parts of this website may not function properly.

Main.instructions: Enter a company name, a service category such as “hairdresser” or “shop”, or a location such as “Dublin” or “Cork” to find Polish businesses in Ireland. You can also leave the field empty to browse all companies.
Main.searchInstructions: Open the map with Polish businesses in Ireland. If you entered a search, the results will be filtered.

Faq.navigate: Use the up/down arrows to navigate between questions, press Enter or Space to expand/collapse an answer, and use Home/End to jump to the beginning/end of the list.
Faq.title: F.A.Q. – Frequently Asked Questions
Faq.subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions.
Faq.subtitleContact: Write to us, we’ll be happy to help.
Faq.search: Search by category, name or location
Faq.question1: What is this website? What is pol.ie?
Faq.answer1: pol.ie is a directory of Polish shops and services in Ireland and Northern Ireland. Each listing includes address, summary and contact details, so you can quickly find what you need.
Faq.answer2: When living abroad you often look for Polish‑speaking services—for language, price or quality reasons. pol.ie shows them all in one place, without switching between multiple sites.
Faq.question3: Another website? There are Facebook groups or Google Maps.
Faq.answer3: Google Maps doesn't highlight Polish businesses, and Facebook groups are full of ads and messy comment threads. pol.ie is a curated, spam‑free catalogue—an addition rather than a competitor.
Faq.question4: How do I add my business?
Faq.answer4: Click “Contact” or “Promote your business”, fill in the short form and we’ll add your listing within 24 hours.
Faq.question5: How much does it cost? Why so expensive?
Faq.answer5: Adding a business is free for now—the site is a passion project, not a business. Paid promotion options may appear later, but browsing the directory will always stay free.
Faq.question6: I’m already listed but want better visibility. Can you move me up?
Faq.answer6: Listings are currently ordered by user interest. Paid highlights are coming soon—if you can’t wait, drop us a message.
Faq.question7: A site for Poles but there’s English—why?
Faq.answer7: Because Irish customers value Polish products and services. An English version opens your business to a wider audience.
Faq.question8: What exactly do I get by adding my business?
Faq.answer8: Extra online visibility. The site has already recorded several thousand views after launch, so early listings get more exposure.
Faq.question9: Does my business have to be registered in Ireland?
Faq.answer9: We don’t require Irish paperwork—just confirm you genuinely operate and provide basic details.
Faq.answer10: We only keep data necessary for the platform. Servers are in the EU and you can view, correct or delete your information at any time.
Faq.question11: My business is on your site without my consent—remove it.
Faq.answer11: No problem. Contact us via the form and we’ll delete the listing within 24 hours. We only use publicly available data.
Faq.question12: My listing has wrong description, tags or location.
Faq.answer12: Send us a message through the contact form describing the corrections. Leave your e‑mail so we can confirm—we'll update it ASAP.
Faq.question13: I don’t have a website or Facebook page. Can you help?
Faq.answer13: Yes. We can enrich your pol.ie card, create a sub‑page or build a full website. Get in touch to discuss.
Faq.answer14: No, it’s optional. You can unsubscribe at any time, but staying subscribed keeps you up to date with promotions and new features.
Faq.question15: I'd like to write an article for the blog. Is this possible, and what should I do?
Faq.answer15: Absolutely! If you have an idea for an article, simply reach out to us through the contact form, and we'll get back to you with the details.
Faq.question16: Why both Irelands if they're two separate countries? What about other parts of the UK?
Faq.answer16: Two countries, one island. The flow of services and people between the Republic and Northern Ireland is seamless, so there's no reason to limit ourselves to just one part. Due to its size and population, most services are located in the Republic of Ireland. A similar service for the rest of the UK is already in the works. Stay tuned!
Faq.question: question
Faq.goToContact: Visit the contact page to ask a question or register your company.
Faq.summaryFaq2: the most frequently asked questions about the service
Faq.summaryFaq3: The questions concern adding companies, costs, features, and data protection. If you do not find an answer to your question, please use the contact form.

HowItWorks.description: You just need to follow 3 simple steps:
HowItWorks.findDescription: Search for Polish businesses by name, category, or location. Use filters to narrow down your options.
HowItWorks.compareDescription: Compare different businesses based on their services, ratings, and locations. Read reviews to make informed choices.
HowItWorks.contactDescription: Contact the businesses directly through their profiles. You can call, email, or visit their websites for more information.
HowItWorks.demoDescription: Explore the map to find Polish businesses in Ireland and Northern Ireland. Click on a marker to view details, navigate, or contact the business.
